note: APPEND-ONLY amendment. grounding-critic-semantic-v1.json and its ledger are immutable and unchanged.

corrections.configurationConclusive: false — candidate C (gemini-3.1-pro) repeat 2 failed transport (llm_strict_not_object), so the configuration/transport dimension did NOT complete cleanly for all candidates.
corrections.eligibilityMeasuredUnderContract: critic-contract-v2 (model echoed factRefs). Eligibility was scored against model-returned provenance, which this sprint replaces with deterministic binding (v3).
corrections.corpusV1Defect: goal_alignment_confound — the single batch goal 'test report loading + accuracy' did not clearly make 'displayed_claim_supported' (a homepage marketing phrase) MATERIAL to the goal, so a critic could correctly withhold support on goal grounds while still recognizing the displayed text.
corrections.displayedClaimMateriality: displayed_claim_supported was not clearly material to the shared report-loading founder goal, confounding the display-vs-truth contrast with goal alignment.

invalidatedConclusions: The claim that Flash-Lite / Pro CONFLATED displayed text with truth is NOT established (the goal confound could explain their rejection of displayed_claim_supported).
The claim that Haiku had PERFECT semantics is NOT established (measured under contract-v2 with the same confound and a factRef-echo requirement).
No production model may be selected from corpus-v1.

telemetrySeparation.eligibilityConclusionConclusive: distinct from configurationConclusive — whether the SEMANTIC eligibility question was conclusively answered, independent of whether the transport/config completed for every candidate.
telemetrySeparation.configurationConclusive: whether the json_schema transport + strict parse completed cleanly for every attempted candidate.

terminologyCorrections.completeCoverage: should distinguish pairPresenceCoverage (every case has a verdict row) from validDecisionCoverage (every case has a valid, bound decision) — v2 uses exactDecisionCoverage.
terminologyCorrections.negativePrecision: the v1 implementation actually computed SUPPORTED/POSITIVE precision (of the 'supported' verdicts, the fraction truly paySafe). v2 renames it supportedPrecision.
